title: quercus lanuginosa subsp. eulanuginosa
intro: Quercus lanuginosa subsp. eulanuginosa, also known as the Chinese white oak or Eulanuginosa white oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus lanuginosa tree, a type of evergreen oak native to China. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Quercus lanuginosa subsp. eulanuginosa include reducing fever, alleviating cough, and treating respiratory tract infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Quercus lanuginosa subsp. eulanuginosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
